content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

March 5, 1990

Ms. Karen L. Brainerd  
Nossaman, Guthner, Knox & Elliott  
915 L Street, Suite 1000  
Sacramento, CA 95814-3701

RE: Your Request for Advice  
Our File No. A-90-039

Dear Ms. Brainerd:

You have requested advice under the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

QUESTION

Would a letter from Senator Roberti soliciting contributions on behalf of Respect Our Animal Rights, a non-controlled committee, violate any provisions of the Political Reform Act or create a presumption of control of the committee by Senator Roberti?

CONCLUSION

The Act does not prohibit an officeholder from soliciting contributions on behalf of a non-controlled committee such as Respect Our Animal Rights. Soliciting funds on behalf of the committee would not indicate control of the committee if this is the only action taken by the officeholder. Enclosed are FPPC advice letters issued to Paul A. Woodruff, No. I-89-180 and Palmer Madden, No. A-85-197 which provide the basis for this conclusion and provide the analysis from which this conclusion was derived.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

FACTS

Senator David Roberti founded and controlled an animal rights political action committee, Respect Our Animal Rights (ROAR). Effective January 1, 1989, Senator Roberti resigned from the Respect Our Animal Rights Board. Nossaman, Guthner, Knox & Elliott believe that a letter from Senator Roberti to ROAR's membership may help generate contributions for the political action committee.

For additional guidance on the issue of what constitutes "control" of a committee, I have enclosed the following advice letters issued by the Commission:

Lyons Advice Letter, No. I-88-451
Pastrick Advice Letter, No. A-87-063
Ferguson Advice Letter, No. A-86-044
Gross Advice Letter, No. A-84-143

Please do not hesitate to contact me at 916/322-5662 if you have additional questions concerning this matter.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Mary Ann Kvasager
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosures
